Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: We need to produce a DuckDB SQL query that does all steps

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: So we need to produce a single query or multiple queries Likely one query using CTEs

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Better approach Use array_agg activity ORDER BY timestamp as variant_array then convert to string via array_to_string

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Then count distinct case_id per variant string Then rank variants by count desc limit K Then filter original events where case_id in select case_id from join

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Lets craft WITH case_variants AS SELECT case_id array_to_string array_agg activity ORDER BY timestamp as variant FROM event_log GROUP BY case_id variant_counts AS SELECT variant COUNT as num_cases ROW_NUMBER OVER ORDER BY COUNT DESC as rn FROM case_variants GROUP BY variant top_k_variants AS SELECT FROM variant_counts WHERE rn less than or equal to K

Name: Validation - PE
Text: This returns all events belonging only to cases whose variants are among top K

Name: Counterfactual Reasoning - IND
Text: Alternatively if want just top K variants sorted by frequency descending

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Join event_log with case_variants and then join with top_k_variants on variant filters only those rows where the computed variant is among top k Thus final result includes all events belonging exclusively to those selected cases
